data.title: Waste Management declares $0.825 dividend
data.description:
• Waste Management (NYSE:WM) declares $0.825/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 1.44%
  • Payable Sept. 26; to shareholders of record Sept. 12; ex-div Sept. 12.
  • The company has now announced a dividend of $0.825 for three consecutive quarters.
  • See WM Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Personalis gains as VA awards work order worth up to $13.5M
data.description:
Shares of Personalis (NASDAQ:PSNL) added ~5% in the premarket on Friday after the cancer test maker announced that it received a new task order worth up to $13.5M under a contract with the Department of Veterans Affairs.

The maker of the NeXT Personal cancer test offers genome sequencing services as part of a contract with the Department of Veterans Affairs Million Veteran Program (VA MVP), a large-scale population sequencing initiative.

The new task order, offered under the VA MVP program, takes effect on September 30, 2025, subject to the receipt of samples and the fulfillment of services by the company, Personalis (NASDAQ:PSNL) said in a regulatory filing on Friday.

To date, the company has received nearly $225 million in task orders under its contracts with the VA MVP, which accounted for 9% of its topline in 2024 and 13% in 2023, respectively.

data.title: Buckle GAAP EPS of $0.89 beats by $0.06, revenue of $305.7M beats by $13.09M
data.description:
• Buckle press release (NYSE:BKE): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.89 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $305.7M (+8.3% Y/Y) beats by $13.09M.
  • Comparable store net sales for the 13-week fiscal quarter ended August 2, 2025, increased 7.3 percent from comparable store net sales for the prior year 13-week period ended August 3, 2024.
  • Online sales increased 17.7 percent to $43.6 million for the 13-week fiscal quarter ended August 2, 2025, compared to net sales of $37.0 million for the 13-week fiscal quarter ended August 3, 2024.

data.title: Fortinet boosts share repurchase program by $1B
data.description:
• Fortinet (NASDAQ:FTNT) has increased its share repurchase program by $1 billion, bringing its aggregate authorized repurchase amount to up to $9.25B.
  • The program was also extended through February 28, 2027. As of August 21, 2025, approximately $1.23B remained available under the existing program, including the $1B increase.

data.title: Hyperscale Data reaffirms 2025 guidance, citing Gresham Worldwide's emergence
BJ’s Wholesale Club reports mixed Q2 results; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• BJ’s Wholesale Club press release (NYSE:BJ): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.14 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $5.38B (+3.3% Y/Y) misses by $100M.
  • Comparable club sales decreased by 0.3% year-over-year due to declining retail prices of fuel.
  • Comparable club sales, excluding gasoline sales, increased by 2.3% year-over-year, led by traffic growth.
  • Membership fee income increased by 9.0% year-over-year to $123.3 million.
  • Member count grew to a record 8 million members.
  • Digitally enabled comparable sales growth was 34%, reflecting two-year stacked comp growth of 56%.

  • The Company provided an updated outlook for fiscal 2025:

    • Comparable club sales, excluding the impact of gasoline sales, to increase 2.0% to 3.5% year-over-year
    • Adjusted EPS to range from $4.20 to $4.35 (prior $4.10 to $4.30) vs $4.31 consensus.
    • Capital expenditures of approximately $800 million

data.title: Bally's lands key regulatory approvals related to the Intralot S.A. deal
data.description:
Bally's Corporation (NYSE:BALY) updated investors on the progress on its previously disclosed transaction agreement with Intralot S.A. (OTCPK:IRLTF) (OTC:IRLTY).

Under the terms of the deal, Intralot (OTCPK:IRLTF) (OTC:IRLTY) is set to acquire all issued and outstanding capital stock of Bally's (NYSE:BALY) Holdings Limited. That subsidiary holds Bally's (BALY) international interactive business. The expected closing of the transaction in Q4 will result in Bally’s (BALY) becoming the majority shareholder of Intralot. The deal is structured so that while Intralot takes direct ownership of the international interactive assets, control of the combined global entity shifts to Bally's (BALY), meaning it will have influence over Intralot's broader operations.

On August 14, Bally's (BALY) received notifications from the Gambling Commission of Great Britain confirming that, contingent on routine ongoing requirements, its UK licenses (held by Gamesys Limited, Bally’s (Newcastle) Limited, and Gamesys Operations Limited) will remain in effect after the transaction closes. The regulatory approval addresses a critical hurdle and clears a path for finalizing the transaction.

The deal with Intralot (OTCPK:IRLTF) (OTC:IRLTY) is seen by analysts as transformative for Bally's (BALY) which has been repositioning itself as a global digital gaming and interactive entertainment player. By integrating with Intralot’s established international operations and technology, Bally’s (BALY) expects to broaden its global reach and strengthen its digital platform, especially in regulated European and emerging markets. 

Intralot (OTCPK:IRLTF) (OTC:IRLTY) is a Greek-based, publicly listed global gaming technology company that provides gambling systems, sports betting management, interactive gaming services, and omnichannel lottery solutions to state-licensed operators in over 39 countries. The company is best known for lottery modernization, digital transformation, and responsible gaming.

Shares of Bally's (BALY) were down 1.6% in premarket trading on Friday.

data.title: Fed's Powell at Jackson Hole: Here's what to watch
data.description:
Investors today are looking to Wyoming, and it's not to book a national park getaway before the summer comes to an end. The annual Jackson Hole Economic Policy Symposium hosted by the Kansas City Fed is underway, with Chairman Jerome Powell set to take the stage at 10:00 AM ET. The theme of the 2025 conference is titled "Labor Markets in Transition: Demographics, Productivity, and Macroeconomic Policy," which will come at a time when labor market indicators are being challenged and questions swirl over real-time economic data.

Snapshot: The recent massive revision to nonfarm payroll numbers not only made waves after President Trump ousted the head of the Bureau of Labor Statistics, but also sent policymakers scrambling to make sense of the new numbers. The 258K jobs that were previously thought to have been added in May and June were actually non-existent, meaning the U.S. only added 19,000 and 14,000 jobs for those two months, respectively. Another weak NFP gain of 73K was later recorded in July, indicating a broad slowdown in job growth for the overall labor market.

Typically, that would have seriously worried many Fed officials (and some are), but others are still debating root causes to determine the path forward for monetary policy. If the underlying reasons are linked to Trump's immigration policies and baby boomer retirement, it might not warrant a strong shift in monetary policy since the downturn is based on labor supply. However, if hiring fundamentals are no longer intact, it could require a bigger dose of monetary stimulus, though that could also be complicated by signs of inflation heating up in the latest CPI and PPI prints.

The big show: Fed Chair Jerome Powell is likely to share some of his thoughts on the matter this morning and what it might mean for the interest rate outlook and long-term policy goals. Wild trading can ensue following the closely watched event, like the notable Jackson Hole hangover of 2022, which saw all three of the major averages slump between 3%-4% after Powell shot down any hopes that the Fed would telegraph a pivot on its aggressive policy tightening. "Equity markets, for its part, are entering this [year's] symposium near their highs, and stocks are largely priced for further confirmation of an easing in rates in September," SA Analyst Justin Purohit noted. "That does leave investors vulnerable if Powell's speech underwhelms and delivers unexpected surprises."

data.title: Self-driving tech company Nuro reels in Nvidia and Uber as strategic investors
data.description:
Autonomous vehicle technology company Nuro closed a $203 million Series E funding round this week to bring its post-money valuation to $6 billion. The funding round featured a select group of high-profile investors, including Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER), Nvidia (NVDA), Baillie Gifford, Icehouse Ventures, Kindred Ventures, Pledge Ventures, T. Rowe Price, Fidelity, Tiger Global Management, Greylock Partners, and XN LP. Of note, Uber (NYSE:UBER) and Nvidia (NVDA) joined as strategic partners for the first time. SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY) is still a significant long-term backer of the California-based company that specialized in last-mile delivery.

Nuro vehicles deliver products in various urban and suburban environments, focusing on both dense metropolitan areas and mid-sized cities in the United States. Its autonomous vehicles currently operate driverlessly in Palo Alto and Mountain View, California, and Houston, Texas. Nuro has attracted attention through its partnerships with companies like Kroger (KR), Domino's Pizza (DPZ), CVS Health (CVS), and 7-Eleven. Recently, Nuro formed a key robotaxi alliance with Uber (NYSE:UBER) and Lucid (LCID).

Nuro was founded in September 2016 by Jiajun Zhu and Dave Ferguson, two former engineers from Google's (GOOG) self-driving car project, which evolved into Waymo. The Mountain View, California-based company initially focused on developing small, electric, self-driving vehicles optimized for last-mile delivery. Nuro gained early attention in 2018 with the launch of its R1 prototype, followed by real-world pilots with major retailers. The company later became the first to receive a federal exemption from the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration for operating autonomous vehicles without conventional controls such as steering wheels or pedals.

Looking ahead, Nuro's long-term vision includes licensing its Level 4 autonomous driving system to OEMs, mobility providers, and even private vehicle manufacturers, aiming to scale its technology across delivery services, ride-hailing fleets, and future personal vehicles.

data.title: ZKH Group Non-GAAP EPADS of -$0.03 in-line, revenue of $302.47M
data.description:
• ZKH Group press release (NYSE:ZKH): Q2 Non-GAAP EPADS of -$0.03 in-line.
  • Revenue of $302.47M, a decrease of 3.7% in the same period of 2024.
  • Non-GAAP EBITDA was negative RMB38.7 million (US$5.4 million), compared with negative RMB47.1 million in the same period of 2024. Non-GAAP EBITDA margin was negative 1.8%, compared with negative 2.1% in the same period of 2024.
